title: In praise of Hosius.

source.title: NPNF2-04. Athanasius: Select Works and Letters

verses.book: Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
verses.text: Of the great Hosius, who answers to his name, that confessor of a happy old age, it is superfluous for me to speak, for I suppose it is known unto all men that they caused him also to be banished; for he is not an obscure person, but of all men the most illustrious, and more than this.

verses.book: Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
verses.text: When was there a Council held, in which he did not take the lead, and by right counsel convince every one?

verses.book: Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
verses.text: Where is there a Church that does not possess some glorious monuments of his patronage?

verses.book: Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
verses.text: Who has ever come to him in sorrow, and has not gone away rejoicing?

verses.book: Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
verses.text: What needy person ever asked his aid, and did not obtain what he desired?

verses.book: Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
verses.text: And yet even on this man they made their assault, because knowing the calumnies which they invent in behalf of their iniquity, he would not subscribe to their designs against us.

verses.book: Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
verses.text: And if afterwards, upon the repeated stripes above measure that were inflicted upon him, and the conspiracies that were formed against his kinsfolk, he yielded to them for a time, as being old and infirm in body, yet at least their wickedness is shewn even in this circumstance; so zealously did they endeavour by all means to prove that they were not truly Christians.
